rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Spiller et al. (2013) to conduct Johnson-Neyman floodlight analyses when decomposing the assortment organization x involvement interaction in Study 3. This citation supports a concrete analytic step (identifying regions where simple effects are significant), not just general background. The paper adopts the cited methodological guidance rather than extending or disputing it.
relation_description: The citing study applies the Spiller et al. floodlight/simple-effects framework to probe moderation effects and determine the involvement ranges where complement-based organization significantly changes purchases and expenditures.
